titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in 2193
GA 2193
The New Testament
Das Neue Testament
The Gospel according to John
john

respStmt.name: the International Greek New Testament Project

titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the International Greek New Testament Project in its work towards the Editio Critica Maior of John between 2010 and 2013.

editorialDecl.p: The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE, later converted to XML. The original transcription guidelines can be found at
The plain text file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used are version 1.4 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Correction at 6.59 deleted by David Parker.
Correction at 6.58 deleted by David Parker.
Correction at 9.21 changed by David Parker.
Corrector tags changed by David Parker and Cat Smith.
Corrections at 6.17, 7.41, 9.18, 10.21, 26, 11.31, 12.6, 40, 47, 14.30 changed by David Parker to make C2 = first hand.
Correction at 7.20 deleted by Rachel Kevern.
[3]ωσκω changed to [γιν]ωσκω at 10.15 by Rachel Kevern.
Empty unclear tags at 7.40, 11.34, 16.4 removed and 2 empty unclear tags in app tags changed to an illegible tag at 13.12 by Rachel Kevern.
Corrector Z tags corrected by Rachel Kevern and David Parker.
Word corrected in 3.30 by David Parker.
Adjusted to XML specifications 1.4 by Catherine Smith.
Converted from plain text by Catherine Smith using Python conversion scripts 1.0.

body.note: Status:G-Α 2193 Level 1 ΤranscriptionUpdated to Level 1: 13.04.09Last modified: 18.03.10Ρrinted transcription checked against printed image: 13.04.09Τhe ΓΡ symbol appears at points throughout this manuscript indicating variant readings.Α V-tag is used in the transcripion at these points.Τhere may have been two different hands that added variant readings.Adjustment of tags and removal of entities for ECM conversion: HAGH 27.3.2013

ab.note: in margin
in margin beside text
in margin beside text
in text and margin
homoeoteleuton
transposition marks used like in 1582
the eta covers two letter spaces
Τhese two additions appear to be done by different correctors, hence separate tags have been used
Αn erased variant reading is not visible on the microfilm
Νote: ειs τελos τoυ βιβλιoυ
Does the point to the note ειs τελos τoυ βιβλιoὺ
homoeoteleuton
some marginal text and a ΓΡ symbol has been erased from the upper margin
layout oυχι αλλ oμoιos to oτι oμoιos
homoeoteleuton
a variant reading may have been erased from the margin
a variant reading may have been erased from the margin
variant but thicker ink so not labelled
correction but thicker ink so not labelled
in text and erased variant reading symbol just visible
corrector deleted o and rewrote it to create space
correction written above the line but now erased
in text, erased variant symbol just visible in margin
has τι been erased;
ει or η
possible correction or note in text
in margin beside text
scribe does not usually use this abbreviation
mark in margin
Is this ευρησει?
direction of correction uncertain
but unusually thick, perhaps not C1
in margin beside text
The p.m. is missing an initial
possibly o to ω
possible above the word
